replies.msg:
繼續操 opinion essay！
睇完題目，呢篇信心大過上次
都係慢慢寫左成個鐘
同埋用左 grammar check 助攻

如果今次ok，下次就挑戰 30分鐘手打
希望香港快d 有 電腦考啦。。。香港真係落後地區

Some people think that it is a good idea to give children a small amount of money each week to spend on what they would like. Other people think that this is wrong and that it is a waste of money.
Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

In recent times, some people believe that by providing the opportunity for children to buy things they want with little money is an ideal practice. It has been argued that this training method is inappropriate as a misuse of money. This essay will examine both sides of this argument and try to reach a conclusion.

There are several reasons why some people today financially support their children to do their shopping. Significantly, It is an experience for the children to understand the concept of cost. It is crucial for children to learn that they must pay for getting the desired return. Furthermore, such spending training helps to build up their self-care skills. From narrow down their selections, to measure the price which is vital for learning the smart way to maximize their satisfaction.

However, there are some drawbacks associated with the parents providing money to children to buy what they want. First of all, children might be falling to the dark side of materialism. It could be worst for their future if they kept money in mind for everything over other virtue like justice and humanity. Besides, most of this shopping is unnecessary and hence may raise toward overbuying habits. Money support by parents every week could force children to think about how to spend the money, instead of what they really need.

In conclusion, although these spending experience could be considered as incorrect training and may even danger their future development, I am of the opinion that the positive effects of such real-world experience which as an early stage training to avoid overspending would be far greater than any of its possible disadvantage.

召喚所有 band 7 - 8.5 神人
求狠批

5 分鐘都唔駛就負評
真係咁差咩

quote.msg: 此回覆已被刪除

replies.msg:
experiences 唔係好少用得到咩？
一般都係用 experience

' experience ' noun singular
' experiences ' noun plural - not often used . 

Oxford Dictionary does not refer to experiences , but Collins Cobuld Dictionary includes 'experiences '

You can say , ' my experiences over many years make me wiser.' Or ' I gained a lot of experience over the years .'

As to your two sentenses , the first is correct without the 's' . The second is OK , but English speakers rarely say it this way . I'd suggest ' she has had a lot of past experience '

replies.msg:
Grammatically, a few problems can be found:

1. Inconsistent use of active and passive voices.

2. Redundant/Unneccessary connectives and sentence structure.

3. Omittion of commas in non-defining relative clauses.

Content-wise, I found it hard to follow your logic flow within the same paragraph. Please don't hesitate from paraphasing or opening a new paragraph where it is appropriate. Try to link your arguments and line of thought more tightly, with the help of concluding, citing facts, expressing personal observations etc.

Overall, the grammar definitely has room for improvement, yet in my humble opinion it does not adversely affect comprehension much. The content is where the problem lies.

replies.msg:
show off？ (你講上一篇就可能係)
一個深字都冇用過。。。

只係跟住人地教 Structural words 嘅 formula 寫。。。
In recent times,
some people believe that ...
However, some people believe that ...
Body: First of all, Besides, Furthermore, secondly ... etc
Although ..., I am of the opinion that ...
In conclusion,
等等
條友自己考 9 9 8.5 9 ...
而且其他人都係教差唔多嘅 template structure



方向應該點講好正確？
Argument + Opinion Essay 我一開波嘅draft：

Pro （use present tense, less modal verbs)
Train them to understand the concept of cost
Build up their self-care skill

Con (use weak vocab)
Falling to a dark side of materialism (wrong)
Most of their buying is unnecessary (waste)

Opinion
Real world practice better than books
Early training will understand and avoid overspending

replies.msg:
我覺得你而家最大既問題係唔識變通、唔識d 文法/句式上nuance啲嘅嘢，雖然學人地用complex sentence，但係都係用錯，所以有種forced/show off-y既感覺

其實我都好難解釋，但係英文好既人/native speaker會睇得出

其實我覺得你與其係度問人、零零星星逐少咁改你都唔會有太大進步，因為你改得今次，下次都好大機會會重複犯同樣既錯，可況連登仔俾既建議其實都唔係全部啱曬

Some people think that it is a good idea to give children a small amount of money each week to spend on what they would like. Other people think that this is wrong and that it is a waste of money.
Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

In recent times, some people believe that by providing the opportunity for children to buy things they want with little money is an ideal practice. It has been argued that this training method is inappropriate as a misuse of money. This essay will examine both sides of this argument and try to reach a conclusion.

There are several reasons why some people today financially support their children to do their shopping. Significantly, It is an experience for the children to understand the concept of cost. It is crucial for children to learn that they must pay for getting the desired return. Furthermore, such spending training helps to build up their self-care skills. From narrow down their selections, to measure the price which is vital for learning the smart way to maximize their satisfaction.

However, there are some drawbacks associated with the parents providing money to children to buy what they want. First of all, children might be falling to the dark side of materialism. It could be worst for their future if they kept money in mind for everything over other virtue like justice and humanity. Besides, most of this shopping is unnecessary and hence may raise toward overbuying habits. Money support by parents every week could force children to think about how to spend the money, instead of what they really need.

In conclusion, although these spending experience could be considered as incorrect training and may even danger their future development, I am of the opinion that the positive effects of such real-world experience which as an early stage training to avoid overspending would be far greater than any of its possible disadvantage.

召喚所有 band 7 - 8.5 神人
求狠批

Grammatically, a few problems can be found:

1. Inconsistent use of active and passive voices.

2. Redundant/Unneccessary connectives and sentence structure.

3. Omittion of commas in non-defining relative clauses.

Content-wise, I found it hard to follow your logic flow within the same paragraph. Please don't hesitate from paraphasing or opening a new paragraph where it is appropriate. Try to link your arguments and line of thought more tightly, with the help of concluding, citing facts, expressing personal observations etc.

Overall, the grammar definitely has room for improvement, yet in my humble opinion it does not adversely affect comprehension much. The content is where the problem lies.

2.
connectives & structural words 我都係跟人學
例如邊d 用我唔好？

3.
講緊邊句？

replies.msg:
Opening:

(generic){Recently.../Nowadays...} or (context dependent){With the evergrowing materialism in developed countries, and even some rising economic powers like China, and India, affluent families tend to provide more and more to their offsprings. This includes but not limited to.... Among which the practice of giving pocket money to children has come to my concern.}

Transition to 1st argument:
{(context dependent)As a teenager who is yet financially dependent, I can definitely see how the act has benefited me./(generic){First and foremost, let us put ourselves into those givers' shoes}

Introduction of parallel arguments for a point:
{Not only ..., but/ Besides/ Not to mention/ What's more, / On top of that... (!presenting stronger or more significant argument!)}

Introduction new points of the same perspective:

{Let's not overlook that/ It cannot be overlooked that/ One should not overlook/neglect the fact that(different styles)} , {On a side note, (less important point)}

Concluding arguments/points:
{Note that / It is crystal clear that/ Notice how [all the aforementioned observations...] / It is irrefutable;cannot be argued;apparent that ...}
